title: Malty Flaky Syrian Creme Fraiche Bake
description: A comforting Syrian dish featuring a flaky crust, malty flavors, and a tangy creme fraiche filling, perfect for breakfast or snack time. This recipe is lacto-vegetarian-friendly and easy to make.

ingredients.name: Malted milk powder
ingredients.notes: For added depth and malty flavor.

instructions: Combine flour, sugar, and malted milk powder in a large bowl. Whisk to incorporate.
Add cubed vegan butter and use a pastry blender or your fingers to work it into the flour mixture until it resembles coarse crumbs.
Gradually add water, mixing until the dough comes together in a shaggy mass.
Knead the dough on a lightly floured surface for 5-7 minutes, until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Wrap the dough in plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes or up to 2 hours.
Roll out the chilled dough to a thickness of about 1/8 inch (3 mm).
Transfer the dough to a parchment-lined baking sheet.
Top the dough with creme fraiche, leaving a 1-inch (2.5 cm) border around the edges.
Sprinkle with tahini, rose water (if using), and confectioners' sugar.
Dust with ground cinnamon and cardamom.
Fold the edges of the dough up over the filling to form a crust.
Bake in the sous-vide machine for 20-25 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown and the filling is set.
Remove from the machine and let cool for 10 minutes before slicing and serving.
Dust with confectioners' sugar and garnish with sesame seeds, if desired.
